requirements.summary: Add client-side search and filter controls to the Dashboard page allowing users to filter incidents by keyword, severity, and status in real time
acceptanceCriteria: A text input field is visible on the Dashboard for keyword search
Typing in the search field filters incidents whose title, description, or affected service contains the entered text (case-insensitive)
A severity filter control (e.g., dropdown or button group) allows filtering by critical, major, minor, or informational
A status filter control allows filtering by investigating, identified, monitoring, or resolved
Filters can be combined (e.g., keyword + severity + status applied simultaneously)
Both the active incidents section and the resolved incidents section are filtered independently
Clearing all filters restores the full incident list
Filter controls are styled consistently with the existing Dashboard design and theme

file_operations.description: Add filter state management (keyword, severity array, status array) using React useState. Insert a filter controls section above the incident lists containing a text input for keyword search, a multi-select or button group for severity filtering, and a multi-select or button group for status filtering. Implement client-side filtering logic that applies all active filters to the fetched incidents array before rendering the active and resolved sections. Ensure both active and resolved incident sections independently apply the same filter criteria. Style the filter controls using existing Tailwind classes and shadcn/ui components to match the current Dashboard theme.
